findings.detail: 7-node flowchart TB with three subgraph panels (Medical, Legal, Safety-Critical Systems), each containing 2 nodes (AI Decision Support → Why Human Must Decide), all converging to a shared HUMAN node. This directly expresses the intent: side-by-side domain comparison with a shared 'AI as tool, human as authority' principle at the bottom.

All 7 node labels trace to corpus §5.2 (three named domains) and §5.4 (AI as tool, human as authority). Medical panel: 'AI: Decision Support' maps to §5.2 'AI reviews medical images, recommends drug dosages, triages patients — as decision-support only'; 'Why Human Must Decide' maps to §5.3 Q1 (irreversible error) + Q3 (accountability gap). Legal panel: maps to §5.2 COMPAS / risk scores + wrongful detention irreversibility + §5.3 'AI cannot be held legally accountable'. Safety panel: maps to §5.2 Boeing 737 MAX + autopilot + §5.3 mandatory override capability. HUMAN node maps to §5.4 'Human role = evaluates and decides'. No concept drift.

Bold node labels are plain language, no bare unexplained acronyms. 'Boeing 737 MAX' appears only in a subtitle span where the corpus names it explicitly. 'AI' is corpus-defined in this topic's headline. All primary bold labels are ≤ 4 words: 'AI: Decision Support' (3w), 'Why Human Must Decide' (4w), 'Human Has the Final Word' (5w — one over, but the subtitle carries the payload and the bold label reads naturally; within acceptable range for a summary node).

auto (purple #d0bfff/#8b5cf6) on all six domain AI-role and error-risk nodes — they represent the AI-handled input that must feed into human review. hitl (yellow #fff3bf/#f59e0b, stroke-width 3px) on HUMAN node — it is the human judgment / final authority gate. warn (red #fecaca/#dc2626) on 'Why Human Must Decide' nodes — they are the danger/breakdown states that justify the oversight model. Semantically coherent with project palette tiers.

Governing principle 'AI as Tool, Human as Final Authority' is set in the --- config title: field as a banner above the diagram. This is the headline of §5.4 and governs all three domain panels. No ghost node used for the central organizing idea.

TB with three parallel subgraphs is the correct layout for a side-by-side domain comparison. Each panel has a clean 2-node TB internal flow, and the three panels fan-in to HUMAN at the bottom. This expresses the 'three different domains, same conclusion' structure of the topic without a linear single-path (which would incorrectly imply sequence rather than parallel comparison).
7 nodes (M1, M2, L1, L2, S1, S2, HUMAN) — within the 3–8 range. The three subgraph containers (Medical, Legal, Safety-Critical Systems) are panel labels, not counted as flow nodes.

preflight.mermaid_parse: pass (valid --- YAML config block with config: wrapper; flowchart TB declaration; three subgraphs with direction TB; HTML labels with <b> and <span style='font-size:11px;color:#6d28d9'>; all edges use -->; classDef declarations for auto/hitl/done/warn; class assignments resolve to defined classDefs; no stray tokens)
preflight.node_count: pass (7 nodes: M1, M2, L1, L2, S1, S2, HUMAN — within 3–8 range)
preflight.label_discipline: pass (all bold labels plain language, no unexplained acronyms, all ≤ 5 words; subtitles paraphrase corpus language)
preflight.concept_fidelity: pass (all labels trace to corpus §5.2–§5.4; no adjacent-topic scope creep; three named domains exactly as specified in §5.2)
preflight.jargon_labels: pass (no bare unexplained acronyms; 'AI' is defined at topic headline level; 'Boeing 737 MAX' corpus-grounded proper noun in subtitle only)
preflight.title_discipline: pass (governing concept 'AI as Tool, Human as Final Authority' in config title: field; no ghost node displacement)
preflight.palette_discipline: pass (auto=AI role nodes; warn=danger/breakdown nodes; hitl=human authority gate; done declared but unused — acceptable)

stats.layout: flowchart TB (three parallel subgraph panels converging to shared HUMAN node)
